preamble: The velar nasal /ŋ/ can undergo a phonetic transformation involving both denasalization and palatalization. This process typically occurs when the velar nasal itself is palatalized or is found in a phonological environment that triggers palatalization. The result is the loss of nasality and the articulation of the sound as a palatalized voiced velar plosive /gʲ/.

examples.note: In the Ardahan local dialect of Turkish, the palatalized velar nasal /ŋ/ undergoes a process of 'resolution', which is documented as a shift towards a palatalized voiced velar plosive.

sources: Demirci, Ü. Ö. (2014). g'li Damak /ŋ/'si ve Bunun Ardahan Yerli Şivesinde Çözülmesi Üzerine. Turkish Studies, 9(6), 275-289.
